# Shared ATS for Small Recruiting Agencies — Developer Tickets (MVP)

<!-- regenerated by amendment 001 (es1=B, es2=A) -->

> Every ticket traces to a business goal. Build order top-to-bottom. Profile: internal-tool. MVP is recruiter-only (client view is v1.1).

## MVP backlog

### T-01 · Create job
As a recruiter I create a job with a title + description so I have a pipeline to work.
- **Acceptance:** A recruiter can create a job; it appears on the shared board for all agency users; a job holds a title + free-text JD.
- **Size:** S
- **Traces to:** g1
- **Depends on:** none

### T-02 · TalentOS adapter + job↔project link
Wire the ATS to TalentOS: one adapter module; creating an ATS job provisions/links a TalentOS project (`ats_job.talentos_project_id`) and PUTs the JD for parsing.
- **Acceptance:** Creating a job creates or links a TalentOS project; the JD is sent and parsed; a contract test pins the TalentOS response shape so an API drift fails loudly.
- **Size:** M
- **Traces to:** g2
- **Depends on:** T-01

### T-03 · Dump resumes into a job
As a recruiter I upload/drag a batch of resumes into a job so they enter the pipeline in one action.
- **Acceptance:** Multi-file upload; each resume becomes a candidate in the "applied" stage; files pushed to TalentOS via the adapter. (MVP assumes manual upload — see gap2.)
- **Size:** M
- **Traces to:** g1
- **Depends on:** T-02

### T-04 · Auto-rank on dump (TalentOS)
When resumes are dumped, orchestrate TalentOS `/process` → `/rank` and cache the `RankingResult` (score, evidence, strengths/gaps, explanation) on each candidate.
- **Acceptance:** After a dump the recruiter sees a ranked shortlist without a manual step; each candidate shows a score + one-line why; ranking runs as one background task per dump (no queue). Hero flow feels < 5 min (s1).
- **Size:** L
- **Traces to:** g2
- **Depends on:** T-03

### T-05 · Pipeline stages (board)
As a recruiter I move candidates across stages (applied → screening → interview → offer) on a shared board.
- **Acceptance:** Drag/move a candidate between stages; state persists in Postgres; all agency users see the same board (concurrent-write safe).
- **Size:** M
- **Traces to:** g1
- **Depends on:** T-03

### T-06 · Candidate drawer (rank evidence)
Open a candidate to see the TalentOS evidence, strengths/gaps, and explanation inline — reused verbatim, no new synthesis.
- **Acceptance:** Drawer shows score + evidence rows + strengths/gaps + explanation; this is the trust surface for the rank (a2). No black-box number.
- **Size:** M
- **Traces to:** g2
- **Depends on:** T-04

### T-07 · Fairness / defensibility surface
Add a fairness tab to the candidate drawer: TalentOS's fairness audit for the ranking + a visible human-override control.
- **Acceptance:** For any ranked candidate the recruiter can view TalentOS's fairness audit and record a human override; overrides are logged. Ships in MVP (es2=A).
- **Size:** M
- **Traces to:** g5
- **Depends on:** T-06

### T-08 · Shared candidate notes
As a recruiter I leave notes on a candidate the whole team can see.
- **Acceptance:** Add/view timestamped, authored notes on a candidate; visible to all agency users.
- **Size:** S
- **Traces to:** g1
- **Depends on:** T-03

### T-09 · Double-contact guard
As a recruiter I get warned when a candidate I'm about to contact is already being worked by a teammate.
- **Acceptance:** Claiming/contacting a candidate sets a visible lock; another recruiter sees a non-blocking inline warning ("X is being worked by Y") with claim/override; overrides are logged.
- **Size:** M
- **Traces to:** g3
- **Depends on:** T-05, T-08

## Deferred (not MVP — do not build yet)
- **Client read-only view (f7)** → v1.1, first fast-follow (ng5) — external authz boundary over candidate PII, sized properly outside the "few weeks" window.
- Job-board auto-posting (ng1) · Interview scheduling (ng2) · Analytics dashboards (ng3) — explicit non-goals.

## Decisions that shaped this backlog
- **es1=B** — recruiter-only MVP; client view is v1.1. Three specialists agreed f1–f6 fits "a few weeks" and f7 does not.
- **es2=A** — fairness audit (T-07) ships in MVP; low cost (TalentOS provides it) and gives the hiring rank a defensibility story.
- Reuse TalentOS via a thin adapter accepted as the ranking architecture (cites ng4/c4; passed the simplifier).
